criteria.criterion: scripts/lib/squeeze.sh defines a function squeeze that collapses runs of spaces to one.
criteria.evidence: scripts/lib/squeeze.sh:3-6 defines `squeeze() { printf '%s' "${1-}" | LC_ALL=C tr -s ' '; printf '\n'; }` — `tr -s ' '` squeezes each run of consecutive ASCII spaces in $1 to a single space.

criteria.criterion: squeeze 'a   b  c' prints 'a b c'.
criteria.evidence: tests/squeeze.sh:20 `assert_squeeze 'a   b  c' 'a b c'` compares $(squeeze "$input") to the expected string; orchestrator evidence shows `bash tests/squeeze.sh` exited 0, so this assertion passed.

tests/squeeze.sh:21 `assert_squeeze '' ''`; with empty input `printf '%s' "" | tr -s ' '` emits nothing and only the trailing newline is printed (stripped by command substitution), and the test run exited 0.

criteria.criterion: tests/squeeze.sh sources the helper, asserts those cases, and exits 0 when all pass.
criteria.evidence: tests/squeeze.sh:5 `source scripts/lib/squeeze.sh`; lines 20-21 assert both spec cases; `set -euo pipefail` (line 2) makes any failed assertion (return 1 at scripts/lib level) abort non-zero, and the last command succeeding yields exit 0 — confirmed by orchestrator: `bash tests/squeeze.sh exited 0`.

scope_finding: Only two new files touched: scripts/lib/squeeze.sh (in scope: scripts/lib/**) and tests/squeeze.sh (in scope: tests/squeeze.sh). No out-of-scope paths (.github/**, specs/**, .orchestrator/**) appear in the diff; orchestrator scope check is PASS.
regression_finding: None observed — the diff adds two new files and modifies nothing existing, so no prior behavior can regress. Minor note (non-blocking): squeeze emits a trailing newline, consistent with 'prints' semantics.
security_findings: None observed — no secrets, no network access (needs_network: false respected), no credential access; shell usage is safe (quoted "${1-}", LC_ALL=C tr on a fixed literal argument, no eval or dynamic command construction).
reasons: All four acceptance criteria are MET with direct evidence in the diff and the orchestrator-run test command exiting 0.
Changed paths are exactly the two in-scope files; integrity and scope checks both PASS.
No security or regression concerns: new self-contained files, safe quoting, no network or secret usage.
